title: Liquid Enterprises
summary: The future of digital business: liquid enterprise concept, changing value creation, plasticity of organisational spaces, and the acceleration of digital linkages.
content_html:
Liquid Enterprises

Digital transformation has progressed through three stages: digital products and infrastructure (late 1990s), digital distribution and web strategy (2000s), and the mobile revolution, social media, and hyper-digitisation (2010s).

The Liquid Enterprise Concept

A liquid enterprise is one where digital technology has fully embedded into all aspects of business -- strategy, operations, supply chains, customer relations, HR. The boundaries of the enterprise have become fuzzy and flexible.

Changing Value Creation

Conventionally, value was created within the boundaries of the organisation through production and consumption of outputs. In the digital economy:

  • Value is created and deployed by multiple channels.
  • Value is created by individual and collective experience.
  • Value creation is non-directly-transactional -- e.g. Google users create value simply by using the platform, generating data even without a financial transaction.

Features of the Liquid Enterprise

  • Value Creation Spaces: No longer limited to the traditional enterprise -- includes competitors (shared platforms), customers, suppliers, and even private employee time.
  • Space-Time Dimension: Ubiquitous digital technology has blurred work and home. Work time is expanding; personal time is shrinking.
  • Plasticity of Organisational Spaces: Organisations are more flexible -- virtual teams, remote work, and changing structures.
  • Valorisation of Data: Data itself has become a key source of value and competitive advantage.
  • Acceleration of Linkages: Connections between organisations, customers, and systems are forming and changing faster than ever.

Implications for Business

Where digital was once an add-on, it is now mainstage and fully embedded. All businesses must continuously assess and reassess how they will transform and compete. Digital business strategy, digitally enabled business models, and fully digital operations are now baseline expectations.

exercises.title: Liquid enterprise definition
exercises.prompt: A liquid enterprise is characterised by:
options: Selling liquid goods and services online
Fully embedded digital technology with fuzzy, flexible organisational boundaries
exercises.answer: Fully embedded digital technology with fuzzy, flexible organisational boundaries

exercises.title: Value creation in digital economy
exercises.prompt: In the digital economy, value creation differs from the traditional model because:
options: Value is created only through direct financial transactions
Value is created through multiple channels including data generation and non-transactional interactions
Value is created exclusively within the firm's boundaries
exercises.answer: Value is created through multiple channels including data generation and non-transactional interactions

exercises.title: Digital transformation stage 1
exercises.prompt: Stage 1 of digital transformation (late 1990s) primarily involved:

exercises.title: Space-time impact
exercises.prompt: The space-time dimension of liquid enterprises refers to:
options: Using GPS to track employee location
Digital technology blurring the distinction between work time and personal time
exercises.answer: Digital technology blurring the distinction between work time and personal time

exercises.title: Data valorisation
exercises.prompt: The feature 'valorisation of data' in liquid enterprises means:
options: Data itself has become a key source of value and competitive advantage
Selling customer data to third parties
exercises.answer: Data itself has become a key source of value and competitive advantage

exercises.title: Value creation spaces
exercises.prompt: In liquid enterprises, value creation spaces now include which unexpected sources?
options: Competitors (shared platforms), customers, suppliers, and even private employee time
exercises.answer: Competitors (shared platforms), customers, suppliers, and even private employee time

exercises.title: Digital transformation today
exercises.prompt: Where digital technology stands in modern business:
options: It is an optional add-on for tech-savvy companies
It is mainstage and fully embedded -- baseline for all businesses
It is only relevant to technology companies
It is managed exclusively by IT departments
exercises.answer: It is mainstage and fully embedded -- baseline for all businesses

exercises.title: Plasticity of organisational spaces
exercises.prompt: The plasticity of organisational spaces in liquid enterprises refers to:
options: More flexible, virtual, and changing organisational structures enabled by digital technology
exercises.answer: More flexible, virtual, and changing organisational structures enabled by digital technology
